description: Configures MFA authentication methods via Entra ID authentication strength policies. Implements defense-in-depth: Standard uses Microsoft Authenticator push, Elevated adds number matching to block MFA fatigue attacks, Strict enforces FIDO2 hardware keys.

risk_profile_effects.standard: MFA method: Microsoft Authenticator push. FIDO2 disabled. SMS enabled.
risk_profile_effects.elevated: MFA method: Microsoft Authenticator number matching (prevents MFA fatigue). FIDO2 enabled. SMS disabled.
risk_profile_effects.strict: MFA method: FIDO2 hardware key (phishing-proof). FIDO2 required. SMS disabled.

risk_profile.label: Risk Profile
risk_profile.client_description: Your security tier. Standard works for most companies. Elevated adds protections against MFA fatigue attacks (where hackers spam approval requests). Strict requires physical security keys — best for high-value targets like finance or executive teams.

mfa_method.label: MFA Method
mfa_method.client_description: Override the MFA method set by your risk profile. Options: 'microsoft_authenticator_push' — User taps 'Approve' on their phone. Convenient but can be vulnerable to MFA fatigue attacks. 'microsoft_authenticator_number' — User types a matching number from screen into their phone. Prevents MFA fatigue. 'fido2' — User taps a physical security key. Completely phishing-proof. Requires hardware keys (~$25-50 each) for all users. Leave blank to use your risk profile default.

enable_fido2.label: Enable FIDO2 / Hardware Keys
enable_fido2.client_description: Enable FIDO2 hardware security key support. Required for Elevated and Strict profiles. Standard-only orgs can skip this. If enabled, users can register YubiKeys or other FIDO2 keys as a second factor.

enable_email_otp.label: Enable Email OTP
enable_email_otp.client_description: Allow email one-time passcode as an authentication method. Useful for guest/external users. Not recommended as primary MFA for internal users.

enable_phone_sms.label: Enable Phone SMS
enable_phone_sms.client_description: Allow SMS-based MFA. Convenient but vulnerable to SIM-swap attacks. Disabled by default for Elevated and Strict profiles.

outputs.auth_strength_policy_id: Use this to reference the authentication strength policy in Conditional Access policies.
outputs.fido2_only_policy_id: Use this to reference the FIDO2-only strength policy. Null if FIDO2 is disabled.
outputs.effective_mfa_method: The actual MFA method in use after resolving risk profile defaults and any overrides.
